name: Current Affairs in Geography (2): River Interlinking & Water Infrastructure

sourceRefs.label: PIB/vajiramandravi.com - Ken-Betwa Link Project; National River Interlinking Programme status (as of July 2025); Jal Jeevan Mission coverage

researchNotes: Second Geography current-affairs topic (2nd lap). The Ken-Betwa Link Project is a good current-affairs anchor since it has a fixed, citable milestone (first interlinking project to enter implementation) with stable supporting numbers, unlike monthly-revised statistics.

reading.intro: Covers India's river-interlinking programme — led by the Ken-Betwa Link Project, the first such project to reach implementation — and the Jal Jeevan Mission's rural water-supply progress.

qa.question: What is the Ken-Betwa Link Project (KBLP), and what milestone does it hold?
qa.answer: It transfers water from the Ken River (Madhya Pradesh) to the Betwa River (Uttar Pradesh) — both tributaries of the Yamuna. It is the FIRST river-interlinking project under the National Perspective Plan (NPP) to enter the implementation stage, with the Prime Minister laying the foundation stone for its key structure, the Daudhan Dam.

qa.question: What are the key infrastructure components of the Ken-Betwa Link Project?
qa.answer: A 221-km canal, a 2-km tunnel, and the Daudhan Dam.

qa.question: What benefits is the Ken-Betwa Link Project expected to deliver?
qa.answer: Irrigation to 10.62 lakh hectares (8.11 lakh hectares in Madhya Pradesh, 2.51 lakh hectares in Uttar Pradesh); drinking water supply to about 62 lakh people; and generation of 103 MW of hydropower plus 27 MW of solar power.

qa.question: What is the scale and structure of India's broader National River Interlinking Programme?
qa.answer: 30 river links identified in total, with a projected cost of ₹5.5 lakh crore — split into the Himalayan Component (14 link projects) and the Peninsular Component (16 link projects). As of July 2025: Pre-Feasibility Reports completed for all 30, Feasibility Reports for 26, and Detailed Project Reports for 11.

qa.question: What is the Jal Jeevan Mission, and what is its current coverage/timeline status?
qa.answer: Launched in 2019 to provide rural households with tap water connections; by June 2026 it had reached about 15.86 crore households (81.94% of rural homes) — the mission has been EXTENDED till 2028 to achieve universal rural tap water coverage.

qa.question: What recent urban-mobility infrastructure milestone did Kochi achieve?
qa.answer: Kochi became the first Indian city to launch a Water Metro Project, integrating inland water transport with urban mobility.

questions.prompt: The Ken-Betwa Link Project transfers water between which two rivers (both tributaries of the Yamuna)?
The Ken-Betwa Link Project holds which distinction among India's river-interlinking projects?
options: It is the first to enter the implementation stage
It is the oldest, dating to 1950
It links the most number of states
